programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A killer gets out of jail on parole and seeks revenge on Steve Sloan, and it appears anyone close to the detective is also in the line of fire. Medical drama, starring Barry Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the murder of an unpopular soap star and must deduce which member of the cast and crew had the strongest motive to commit the crime. Starring Dick Van Dyke and Ian Ogilvy(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Stewart is selected to be part of a group of men known as LA's most eligible bachelors, but is horrified to discover the chosen few are being systematically killed - and after examining puncture marks on the victims' necks, Sloan has to explore some unorthodox theories(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan links a suspicious suicide to a dating agency, prompting Jack to go undercover to investigate - but he must take the deception further than anticipated to crack the case. Mystery drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The murder of a volleyball player reveals how much her team-mates disliked her, and a mysterious stalker only serves to complicate matters further for the sleuthing medic. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Victoria Rowell and Barry Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: An investigation into the evidence against Darryl Pinkins and Roosevelt Glenn, raising questions about the technology used decades ago(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: A look into the case of Dr Steven Schwartz, a beloved Florida physician who ended up dead in his Tarpon Springs mansion(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Paige's estranged mother enlists the team's help when she accidentally uncovers a forgotten nuclear reactor, which is on the verge of exploding. Drama inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must rescue a woman trapped in the La Brea Tar Pits before she runs out of oxygen, while Paige's mother Veronica maximises Ralph's troop cookie sales(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team encounters gunrunners while spending Christmas Eve at an electronics-free mountain getaway, and Tim makes a decision about his place in the unit(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Cabe is seriously wounded by exploding shrapnel during a desert mission, and the team must freeze his body in order to save his life. Crime drama, starring Robert Patrick(n)

programme.desc: Amanda's frightened friend does a disappearing act shortly after giving birth to quadruplets, leaving a flustered Jack Stewart holding the babies - and Dr Sloan investigating the activities of an illegal adoption ring. Dick Van Dyke stars(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm, Mac and Bud investigate a fatal accident involving members of a jinxed squadron to prove that supernatural powers are not at work(n)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm and Mac face each other in court over the case of a Navy Seal who killed a colleague while they were working together on a mission(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A millionaire former CIA man meets a premature end when someone plants a controlled explosive in his lighter. Over the years he had built up an impressive list of enemies, so finding his killer looks like an uphill task, but as the agents start working through the potential culprits, a recovered computer file leads them to an escort service used by a wanted hitman. However, what they don't bank on is the chief suspect turning up dead(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents are alerted to an armoured-car heist in which five men stole $70million in gold bars and killed two armed guards in the process. When Callen and the team investigate, they encounter the dead bodies of all but one of the robbers along with the entire seized haul - but is their luck too good to be true? Meanwhile, Granger partners up with a reluctant Kensi. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. They soon discover that a complicated murder has taken place and identify a pair of suspects. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks' one-time LAPD informant Jenny Radler is kidnapped after uncovering dodgy dealings linking a lucrative car shipping outfit to a global terrorist ring. Facing a race against time, Kensi poses as a streetwise car thief to prove Jenny's findings, while the rest of the team members try to locate her before it's too late. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate the mysterious death of a Marine in Afghanistan, urged on by the victim's distraught wife, who is adamant the Navy is hiding something from her. programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to the heart of the Snowdonia National Park to explore its Slate Trail, an 83-mile loop taking in some of the area's least-trodden path, while Ellie Harrison is in the foothills of Llanberis to find about about the Outdoor Partnership. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds

Meanwhile, Amy and Frederick are at the Admiralty, looking for George and trying to help organise a response. As the invasion continues, Frederick decides they should go to his summer house in Shoeburyness, and there they head to the beach to try to catch a boat away from Britain. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Meat: A Threat to Our Planet?
programme.desc: Animal biologist Liz Bonnin investigates the environmental impact of animals being raised to supply the world's increasing demand for meat, and looks at efforts reduce the effects. She begins her journey in Texas, on a vast intensive farm with 50,000 cows, which emit huge amounts of methane, an incredibly potent greenhouse gas that is contributing to climate change. At a university in California, she hears of the efforts of scientists to better understand a cow's digestion so that they can reduce methane emissions(n)

Pixar animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Owen Wilson and Michael Caine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Cooper Clarke and Phill Jupitus embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge in East Anglia, as well as taking a look at the Norwich School of 19th-century artists(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to prepare a croque monsieur in 15 minutes for Marcus Wareing, while Monica Galetti sets three more the task of making pea risotto. The six contestants move on to the Signature Dish stage, where they must inspire both judges with their own unique creation before three are chosen to go through to the quarter final and three leave the contest(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to cook monkfish with vermouth sauce for Marcus, while three more chefs make a crepe souffle with a caramel sauce for Monica. For the second challenge, the contestants have to prepare a dish of their own design to impress the judges and earn their place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Properties in St Albans, Hertfordshire, are judged by neighbours, beginning with a 1930s space-saving conversion, a Victorian terrace, and a traditional two-up, two-down(n)

programme.title: The Wedding Planner (2001)
programme.desc: A single woman who organises weddings for a living is swept off her feet by a dashing doctor, but her dreams of him becoming her husband suffer a setback when she discovers she has already been hired to organise his marriage to another woman. Romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Matthew McConaughey, Bridgette Wilson-Sampras, Justin Chambers and Joanna Gleason(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin visit an island in Norway, spending two days in a house built on a footprint of just 100 square metres. In Spain, the pair head to a home built into a steep cliff face overlooking the Mediterranean, featuring a cantilevered terrace offering maximum sea views and a swimming pool as well as an unusual tiled roof. After viewing a house in New Zealand crafted from two separate wooden cladded structures, the duo explore a home in Canada inspired by two ships in dry dock, designed to peer over the coast, allowing the sea to pass underneath(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores Britain's connection to the fjords of Norway, with Neil Oliver visiting Bergen, which was once the country's commercial capital. Mark Horton meets shipwrights who build boats using the centuries-old methods of the Vikings, and Nick Crane discovers how the movement of ice at Geirangerfjord will one day cause a devastating tsunami(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: The documentary follows throughout spring. New life begins on Skokholm off the western tip of Pembrokeshire as pufflings hatch, each of which will need to eat its own weight in fish every day. Two hermit crabs do battle on Rhossili Beach, lapwing chicks are targeted by crows after an easy meal and the eel-like sea lampreys move up into the Wye to breed(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by rail through Queensland, marvelling at the engineering expertise of a railway line built in the 1890s. He visits a plantation where he learns how to harvest sugar cane, finds out how the townsfolk of Innisfail withstand the tropical storms that strike the region and ventures into the hinterland, where he braves a crocodile safari. At Mungalla Cattle Station, he hears how aboriginal people faced brutality at the hands of 19th-century settlers and after a singalong, he makes tracks for his end destination of Townsville - the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Flamengo v River Plate (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the 60th Copa Libertadores final, held at Estadio Monumental in Lima, Peru. Argentinian outfit River are the holders, having defeated Buenos Aires rivals Boca Juniors in last year's final, and again eliminated them this year in the semi-finals. Meanwhile, Flamengo's last-four match was an all-Brazilian affair, which saw them thrash Gremio 6-1 on aggregate to maintain their hopes of lifting this trophy for the first time since 1981. With analysis from Gilberto Silva and commentary by Mark Scott(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The documentary explores how novels through the ages have embraced the class struggle, from Dickens, Gaskell and Hardy through to Robert Tressell's The Ragged-Trousered Philanthropists and the group of working-class writers who began to write their own stories in post-war Britain. With readings by Russell Tovey, Darren Boyd, Alex Macqueen, Emily Berrington, Freddie Fox, Rian Gordon and Olivia Barrowclough(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: With Blanca grieving for a sudden loss, Elektra takes it upon herself to organise an out-of-town trip for the girls. Starring MJ Rodriguez and Dominique Jackson(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: A new ball category is introduced that stirs up old traumas for Pray Tell. Blanca struggles to pick up the pieces and reassemble the troops following a health setback. US drama, starring MJ Rodriguez and Billy Porter(n)

programme.title: Before the Winter Chill (2013)
programme.desc: A surgeon has an unexpected encounter with a mysterious woman claiming to be a former patient. When she sends a bouquets of roses to his house to thank him for helping her, the doctor's wife suspects he is harbouring secrets, and their seemingly perfect marriage starts to fall apart. Drama, starring Daniel Auteuil and Kristin Scott Thomas. In French(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: Bernard summons Malachia to question him about the occurrences in the Abbey. Adso is bemused when he sees Salvatore with a cat in a sack and is told it is for a love potion. Remigio the cellerer is surprised when Anna arrives at the abbey and recognises her as the daughter of the heretic Dolcino and she asks him for her father's letters which he had been asked to keep safe. Severinus's body is found in his laboratory and Remigio is the prime suspect, but William and Adso have little time to search for a strange book that Severinus told them about. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter B, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke help Claire and Richard in Barnsley. They are desperate for somewhere to socialise and reconnect with friends and family, following difficult fertility treatment. After a day of hard work, hopefully their revamped garden will work wonders(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don takes his global tour to the US to investigate whether the world's most powerful nation expresses its wealth through its beauty spots. In New York he meets a group of garden guerrillas transforming derelict land into community oases. In Virginia, he visits a conservation project that details America's history, and in California he searches for plots that reflect the glamour of Hollywood(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to the Balcaskie Estate in the East Neuk of Fife, where Charlotte Smith tries her hand at competitive ploughing, Ellie Harrison meets some local food producers, and Steve Brown is at Pittenweem harbour as the fishing boats bring in their catches. Meanwhile, it's a tense time for Adam Henson down on the farm as his cattle are tested for TB(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses coffee to its full potential. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Tom Parker Bowles and Grace Dent. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef explores Burgundy and the Auvergne, sampling a sorrel soup made by a chateau owner from the produce of the garden and showcasing Salers beef and Cantal cheese. Back in his Provencal country kitchen, Rick prepares Cantal Bourriols - buckwheat pancakes made with farmhouse eggs and Cep mushrooms(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Musician pals Emeli Sande and Naughty Boy seek out antiques with the help of auctioneers Catherine Southon and Charlie Ross. Alon the way they discover the story of the Scottish connection at Kew Gardens and pull out all the stops on a mighty Wurlitzer cinema organ. But who will be victorious at the auction?(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profile of Oscar-winning actor John Mills, featuring clips from TV appearances he made during his seven-decade career, and capturing the milestones and highlights of his fame. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (1946)
programme.desc: An orphan falls in love with the cold-hearted adopted daughter of an eccentric old woman. A mysterious benefactor gives him the chance to rise through London's high society, but in the process he forgets his humble roots and is not prepared for the truth about the girl he loves and the stranger funding his new life. Drama, based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring John Mills, Jean Simmons, Finlay Currie and Alec Guinness(n)

programme.title: Golf: World Tour Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the season-ending European Tour event, held at Jemeirah Golf Estates in Dubai(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: For every pound spent on food shopping, 77p goes to the supermarkets, giving them a huge influence over what people eat. In an experiment to discover the hidden truths about everyday foods, Horizon has built a supermarket where the products are labelled with the real story of how they are produced and their effect on consumers and the environment. Mathematician Hannah Fry and dietician Priya Tew invite members of the British public to come in and discover the truth about their favourite foods(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness take over as the latest hosting team of the motoring show. Their first episode takes them to Ethiopia, where they test cars to see how they can endure in the searing heat. Back at the track, Chris puts the Ferrari 488 Pista and McLaren 600LT through their paces(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the business plans of two former bankers who have launched a range of beach towels, an entrepreneur whose family-run company makes novelty plush goods, and two old friends who have come up with a form of packaging based around air. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates visits Beijing, and learns about the life of President Xi Jinping and his attitudes on the relationship between Communism and capitalism. He discovers the Chinese social media platforms that have sprung up in the absence of banned Western sites and talks to some of the city's wealthiest and most impoverished citizens(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Ethiopia, where anthropologist Mike Tamerat shows him a side of the country in stark contrast to the common public image of a nation devastated by famine. Romesh experiences local traditional medicine and jazz music, as well as learning more about the country's oppressive political regime that has led to decades of turmoil for a country that stands on the brink of a new era in its history(n)

programme.title: Goldstone (2016)
programme.desc: Detective Jay Swan's investigation of a young woman's disappearance causes friction in an Outback township under a mining company's sway. Local cop Josh Waters is growing used to looking the other way over everyday corruption, while Aboriginal elder Jimmy sees Jay has a deeper link to the territory. Thriller, starring Aaron Pedersen, Alex Russell, David Wenham and David Gulpill(n)

programme.title: Paths of Glory (1957)
programme.desc: A French assault on a German position during the First World War ends in disaster, but the general in charge refuses to accept the plan was doomed from the start and demands that three men from the ranks be executed as an example to the rest. However, the colonel who led the attack resolves to defend the condemned soldiers. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Kirk Douglas and Adolphe Menjou(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Bolton, Greater Manchester, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties from Leicester are under the spotlight - a 100-year-old Dutch barge, a modern mansion and a Scandinavian barn(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Helen bravely admits her true feelings, and Lofty tries to let her down gently - but neither of them is prepared for a tragic incident that tears their world apart. A troubled Elliot tries to rally support for Jac ahead of Darwin's Mortality and Morbidity review, and Xavier's conscience is pricked when an old acquaintance is admitted, and begins to reconsider his working relationship with Donna(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea is delighted to work on a 1950s toy bike gifted to the owner's brother by comedy duo Laurel and Hardy, during a performance in their home town. The pedals have seized up and rust has taken hold of the body work. Other items worked on include a pair of beloved boxing gloves and a shattered ostrich egg cigarette box. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: The work of nine mountain vets, several nurses and stable hands in three very different veterinary practices located within the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland, as they care for a diverse range of animals in clinics, on farms and smallholdings. The Mourne area is well known for its stunning natural beauty and traditional country life, and the trio of practices respond to over 100 clinic cases and call-outs a day. In this episode, the founder of the oldest vet practice in Downpatrick, Maurice King, receives a call about an extraordinarily rare birth on a farm he knows well(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music(n)

programme.title: Half a Sixpence (1967)
programme.desc: A humble draper's assistant in Edwardian London unexpectedly inherits a fortune from his grandfather. In his newly minted circumstances, he starts to drift away from his old friends in favour of the company of high-society swells, and upset his girlfriend when his head is turned by a lady from the upper classes. Musical based on HG Wells' Kipps, starring Tommy Steele, Julia Foster, Cyril Ritchard and Penelope Horner(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Memorable moments and previously unseen finds, including a handkerchief commemorating cricket legend Dr WG Grace and three postcard albums found on a council tip(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off. Plus, in-depth analysis of the weekend's performances(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former Love Island stars Wes Nelson and Georgia Steel join experts Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell in North Yorkshire on a quest for items to sell at an auction in North Shields. On a break from antique hunting, Wes takes Izzie to Scarborough Spa to hear about the resort's remarkable history, while the other pair go for a trip on a very old vessel(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Journeymen and the Junipers return for one last chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Malcolm I of Scotland, John Bradman, Kiefer Sutherland, and Ivanka Trump(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: Oskar is called away from Max and Clara's engagement party to investigate the brutal murders of three women in a brothel. Clara understands Max's eagerness to join the investigation and allows him to follow, much to the detective's dismay. The police almost immediately arrest Victor Krull, a man who regularly visited the brothel and took a particular interest in one of the victims, whose body has been washed and ritualistically displayed. The press are informed of the arrest, but Max is not at all convinced(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Bollywood: The World's Biggest Film Industry
programme.desc: Anita Rani concludes her exploration the world of Indian cinema by examining how a new generation of Bollywood stars are challenging the industry to keep up with the country's unusually young population - more than half the 1.3 billion people in India are under 25. She also investigates why Indian cinema is so fascinated with having westerners on screen, and visits one of Asia's largest slums to find out about an increasing trend in Bollywood to use the enormous power of cinema to bring about social change(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences(n)

programme.title: James May's Big Trouble in Model Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ken, head of audio development, is sent on a top-secret mission to capture the sound of a steam locomotive for its potential use in a new model, while marketing and development director Simon Kohler takes on two of his biggest rivals in a dramatic showdown. Plus, the company takes on its first female product designer in the shape of Caitlin Williams(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. A boy's future hangs on the outcome of a single horse race in the vast grasslands of the Steppe, and in a European salt marsh, a young man duels with a savage bull in a centuries-old battle of wills(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry Ambrose is asked a favour by Heather Novack, the newly promoted daughter of his old friend Jack. She asks him to help her investigate the mysterious deaths of two people in a motel room in Harry's home town of Keller in upstate New York. Return of the crime drama, starring Bill Pullman, with Natalie Paul and Tracey Letts(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: As she and Ambrose begin to discover more about Julian and the events at the motel, Heather is shocked to learn that the boy had been living in Mosswood, a notorious commune on the outskirts of Keller. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman, Natalie Paul and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: Stereophonics: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton introduces a performance by the rock band, featuring tracks from their recent number one album Kind alongside songs from their back catalogue, including Dakota, Have a Nice Day and Mr Writer(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Surreal comedy with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring sketches, slapstick, songs and stunts, accompanied by a series of special guests. In the first of the four-part series, Vic and Bob disrupt a couple's cosy dinner date with a song and dance routine, before the comedians are interrupted by Tom Cruise performing a daring stunt(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic Reeves debuts his new act for the Royal Variety Performance, before he and Bob Mortimer embark on a ghost hunting experience in a disused toilet. Plus, the return of singing duo Mulligan and O'Hare, and an exclusive preview of a new album from Andrew Neil and Professor Robert Winston(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer are joined by pop singer George Ezra, who proves he is a dab hand with a hammer and nails, and Bob shows Vic his brand new, on-trend wig. Later, the pair embark on another spooky ghost hunt, this time in the disused dressing rooms of some of the BBC's biggest television stars(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer showcases his incredible new dance routine and eventually forces a reluctant Vic Reeves to join in. Plus, characters Donald and Davey Stott take to the stage to perform an ambitious magic routine, and The Man With The Stick makes a long awaited return, before Vic and Bob end the show with a performance of their song, You Can Do It(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Nicky Campbell present the edition first aired on November 3, featuring performances by Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Robert Palmer, Tanita Tikaram, Bryan Ferry, Robin Beck, Kylie Minogue, Yazz, Enya and Guns N' Roses(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Sybil Ruscoe present the edition first aired on November 10, featuring performances by Brother Beyond, Bryan Ferry, Robin Beck, Salt-N-Pepa, INXS, Chris de Burgh, Deacon Blue, Enya and Prince.(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits a secret location in the Cotswolds, which appears to be a high-status Anglo Saxon cemetery, where she finds a very precious and fragile artefact(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Liverpool at Meadow Park and Bristol City v Manchester City at Stoke Gifford Stadium(n)

programme.title: The River Taff with Will Millard
programme.desc: Writer and fisherman Will Millard reflects on how the waterway is now one of the finest trout and salmon rivers in Wales, having once ran black with coal dust. He meets the members of the Lewis Merthyr Colliery Brass Band and fishes for grayling with a former miner who is now a champion fly fisherman. Will also visits one of Britain's biggest open cast coal mines and sees how the landscape is now being re-claimed after centuries of mining(n)

programme.title: Fair Isle: Living on the Edge
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary charting life on Britain's most remote inhabited island, which is home to 55 people and situated halfway between mainland Shetland and the Orkneys. A new couple settle in and adapt to island life, and cameras follow a family whose 11-year-old son has to leave home to board at secondary school on mainland Shetland(n)

programme.title: Kill Your TV: Jim Moir's Weird World of Video Art
programme.desc: The comedian, aka Vic Reeves, explores video art, revealing how different generations hacked the tools of TV to pioneer new ways of creating work that could be beautiful, bewildering and experimental. He examines how this outsider art form became part of the creative establishment, as well as a purpose-built platform for a screen-obsessed world(n)

programme.title: Secrets of British Animation
programme.desc: Documentary exploring animation in the UK from the Victorian era to the present day, taking in the work of Bob Godfrey, John Halas & Joy Batchelor, Len Lye and Aardman Animations, and examining the ingenious methods that low-budget British productions have used to create their own distinctive styles(n)

programme.title: A Grand Night In: The Story of Aardman
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a celebration of the animation studio, revealing how Morph, Shaun the Sheep and Wallace and Gromit first came to life. Featuring contributions by David Tennant, Hugh Grant, Martin Freeman and many more who have provided the voices of Aardman's array of characters(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A chronicle of New York's drag scene in the 1980s, focusing on balls, voguing and the ambitions and dreams of those who gave the era its warmth and vitality(n)

programme.title: America's Greatest Opera House: The Story of the Met
programme.desc: The creation of the Metropolitan Opera's home of the last 50 years, focusing on the artists, architects and politicians who shaped the cultural life of New York in the '50s and 60s. Among the notable figures in the film are famed soprano Leontyne Price, who opened the new Met in 1966 in Samuel Barber's Antony and Cleopatra, and Rudolf Bing, the Met's general manager, who engineered the move from the old house to the new one(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore describes how the Habsburgs transformed Vienna into a multi-national city of music, culture, art and ideas. Simon explores the Habsburgs' rise to power and discovers more about how Vienna marked Europe's front line in the struggle to defend both Christendom and the Catholic Church from revolutionaries who plotted to destroy them(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Danish director Mads Brugger and Swedish private investigator Goran Bjorkdahl, examine the circumstances surrounding the death of UN secretary-general Dag Hammarskjold in a plane crash in 1961. Scores of false starts, dead ends, and elusive interviews later, Brugger and Bjorkdahl begin to sniff out something more monumental than anything they'd initially imagined(n)

programme.title: Tales from the Tour Bus: Rock 'n' Roll on the Road
programme.desc: Rick Wakeman offers a first-hand account of rockers on the road from the late 1950s onwards, recalling the golden age of touring - a secret history of transport cafes, transit vans, B&Bs, sleepless roadies and loved ones left at home. The musician and tour bus aficionado also reflects on the audiences, both good and bad, and the gigs themselves, from the early variety package to the pubs, clubs and stadiums. With contributions by Suzi Quatro and members of Dr Feelgood, the Shadows, the Pretty Things, Fairport Convention, Happy Mondays, Aswad, Girlschool and the Damned(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy, Buster, and Binky argue over the design for a new mini golf hole(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun becomes tired of eating grass and tries to steal one of the farmer's apples, but all of his attempts are thwarted by a sneezing accomplice(n)

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Revival of the classic cartoon about the adventures of a crimebusting rodent and his sidekick Penfold. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Walter launches his own mega-dull, hypnotic TV channel with an signal booster that swamps all other channels(n)

programme.title: MOTD Kickabout
programme.desc: Football show featuring stars from the worlds of sport and entertainment, focusing on everything from school teams all the way up to the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: Can You Kick It?
programme.desc: The remaining finalists travel to the home of the European champions to receive coaching at Anfield, before taking on the Extreme Drone Drop. Chelsea star Erin Cuthbert returns(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Maariya was scared of going underwater, but overcame her fear a few years ago(n)

programme.title: The Playlist
programme.desc: Big names from the world of pop introduce a selection of most-requested tracks, as decided by viewers at home, while Greg James unveils the official weekly UK top 10 chart(n)

programme.title: Max & Harvey: FOMO
programme.desc: Max and Harvey are joined by Blue Peter's cute dog Henry to put their training skills to the test. If you love lots of adorable animals, this is definitely the episode for you!(n)

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Series about the human body, exploring how it works and how it recovers when injured(n)

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Ms O gets kicked out of her office due to odd infestations(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Strange Hill High
programme.desc: Mitchell causes havoc when he cheats on a maths exam, and the gang needs to defrost a boy genius who was frozen years ago to fix the problem(n)

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: A baby giraffe is struggling to live up to his father's expectations of what a son should be(n)

programme.title: Diddy TV

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at the Etihad Stadium and Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park. Plus, action from Arsenal v Southampton at the Emirates Stadium, Bournemouth v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at the Amex Stadium, Everton v Norwich City at Goodison Park, Watford v Burnley at Vicarage Road and West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium. programme.desc: There is excitement in the air as the Sainsbury's staff prepare for a party to celebrate the supermarket's 150th year of trading, especially as the Queen will be there. After 50 years' service on the tills, Jenny from the Wallington store has been selected to meet Her Royal Highness and she cannot wait. As the supermarket celebrates its past, it also looks to the future by trialling an experiment to remove all the checkouts from one store, encouraging customers to pay with their mobiles. Will it change the face of shopping or simply send customers elsewhere?(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: George searches frantically for Amy, but encounters more capsules and soon realises that the scale of the invasion is beyond his comprehension. Meanwhile, Amy and Frederick are at the Admiralty, looking for George and trying to help organise a response. As the invasion continues, Frederick decides they should go to his summer house in Shoeburyness, and there they head to the beach to try to catch a boat away from Britain. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Sheffield United v Manchester United. Mark Chapman presents action from today's only Premier League fixture, held at Bramall Lane. The Blades have made an impressive start to their first top-flight campaign since the 2006/07 season, and will have been hoping to take the scalp of Ole Gunnar Solskjaer's men, who have struggled on their travels. With analysis by Darren Fletcher and Tim Cahill(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Liverpool at Meadow Park and Bristol City v Manchester City at Stoke Gifford Stadium(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass return to tackle more potential miscarriages of justice as they re-examine historic murder cases on behalf of the family of those convicted. In the first edition they investigate their oldest case yet, the murder of a canal boat passenger 180 years ago in which boatmen were convicted and publicly hanged(n)

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts follow the development progress of a tenement flat in Glasgow, a two-bedroom terrace in Birmingham and a house in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Counterfeit contact lenses that are not sterile and risk wearers eyesight and the fake website racket that conned innocent Brits out of tens of millions of pounds(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: The daily lives of two horse vets working from their clinic in Carmarthenshire. Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes face an uncooperative wild Welsh mountain pony stallion who needs sedating before an operation, an elderly horse with a wobbly tooth and a champion mare with a badly gashed leg facing emergency treatment(n)

Louise is convinced Lisa is unwell after seeing her medication - but when Keanu tells Sharon, she is left feeling guilty. Ian holds an event to plug his Walford councillor campaign, only for his reputation to be compromised by an unwelcome visitor, and Ben is in high spirits when he manages to help Sonia(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The team visits Wales' biggest charity shops to find out what is best to donate and to buy. Presented by Lucy Owen, Omar Hamdi and Rachel Treadaway-Williams(n)

programme.title: Meat: A Threat to Our Planet?
programme.desc: Animal biologist Liz Bonnin investigates the environmental impact of animals being raised to supply the world's increasing demand for meat, and looks at efforts reduce the effects. She begins her journey in Texas, on a vast intensive farm with 50,000 cows, which emit huge amounts of methane, an incredibly potent greenhouse gas that is contributing to climate change. At a university in California, she hears of the efforts of scientists to better understand a cow's digestion so that they can reduce methane emissions(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: With a huge storm due to hit Wales, the marketing team comes up with the idea of sponsoring it as a publicity stunt. Meanwhile, Nez develops an accommodation app, but due to a combination of the bad weather, a technical mix-up and no alternative sleeping options, Elaine ends up using it and has to spend the night with him in his one-bedroom flat(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the second episode, brother and sister Simon and Sarah from Bristol, friends Rita and Angela from London, and grandson and grandad Alex and Steve from Port Talbot in Wales take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman return to the Elstree studio after last week's trip to Blackpool, which saw Michelle Visage and Giovanni Pernice leave the contest. As well as the usual ballroom battle, judge Bruno Toniolo will be treating the audience to a rendition of Can't Take My Eyes Off You, accompanied by the a dance number by the professionals(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian returns with his evening entertainment show, recorded in front of an audience at the London Palladium. The first edition features a musical performance by Liam Payne, who performs his latest single, Stack it Up, and survival expert Bear Grylls hands his phone over to the host for Send To All(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The truth about Lev's past makes Ruby feel uncomfortable. Meanwhile, a surprise visit from her mother at the hospital puts Rosa and David's fake relationship to the test, and after dealing with a patient who brings back traumatic memories from the terrorist attack, Ethan decides he needs to get away(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at the Etihad Stadium and Crystal Palace v Liverpool at Selhurst Park. Last season's corresponding fixture between the Citizens and Blues ended in a 6-0 rout for the home side, with Sergio Aguero hitting his 11th top-flight hat-trick in the process. Plus, action from Arsenal v Southampton at the Emirates Stadium, Bournemouth v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Vitality Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at the Amex Stadium, Everton v Norwich City at Goodison Park, Watford v Burnley at Vicarage Road and West Ham United v Tottenham Hotspur at London Stadium. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Houston Texans v Indianapolis Colts at NRG Stadium(n)

programme.title: What Doesn't Kill You (2008)
programme.desc: Two childhood friends become criminals in the employ of a Boston mobster, leading one to develop a drug habit that nearly costs him his wife and children. After a spell in prison, he resolves to make a fresh start as a devoted family man, but his friend tempts him with the promise of an armoured car robbery. Crime drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Ethan Hawke and Amanda Peet(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sioned Young and her family embark on a trial week living in Perth, but husband Richard is concerned about the practicalities of such a huge change from their lives in North Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown hosts the first of two programmes to find the Gospel Choir of the Year, with five amateur groups taking to the stage at Reading Town Hall. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset, and also visits Dunkeswell Aerodrome(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble watch as a gorilla undergoes surgery, while Jean Johansson finds out just how much the baby meerkats have grown when she tries to catch them(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to the heart of the Snowdonia National Park to explore its Slate Trail, an 83-mile loop taking in some of the area's least-trodden path, while Ellie Harrison is in the foothills of Llanberis to find about about the Outdoor Partnership. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results, leaving the two couples with the lowest combined scores to take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Opera star Andrea Bocelli provides the music(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and the Gyptians arrive in the North and seek the help of the Witches' Consul, as well as Texan aeronaut Lee Scoresby and a bear who has been working in a scrapyard for food and board since being tricked out of his armour. Fantasy adventure, starring Dafne Keen and Lin-Manuel Miranda(n)

programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sioned Young and her family embark on a trial week living in Perth, but husband Richard is concerned about the practicalities of such a huge change from their lives in North Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams search for well-priced antiques and collectibles to sell at auction in the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, with the assistance of experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown hosts the first of two programmes to find the Gospel Choir of the Year, with five amateur groups taking to the stage at Reading Town Hall. They have to impress vocal coach Mark De-Lisser, West End star Brenda Edwards and pop singer Rick Astley, who will pick the winner(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset, and also visits Dunkeswell Aerodrome(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble watch as a gorilla undergoes surgery, while Jean Johansson finds out just how much the baby meerkats have grown when she tries to catch them(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to the heart of the Snowdonia National Park to explore its Slate Trail, an 83-mile loop taking in some of the area's least-trodden path, while Ellie Harrison is in the foothills of Llanberis to find about about the Outdoor Partnership. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results, leaving the two couples with the lowest combined scores to take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Opera star Andrea Bocelli provides the music(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra and the Gyptians arrive in the North and seek the help of the Witches' Consul, as well as Texan aeronaut Lee Scoresby and a bear who has been working in a scrapyard for food and board since being tricked out of his armour. Fantasy adventure, starring Dafne Keen and Lin-Manuel Miranda(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: George searches frantically for Amy, but encounters more capsules and soon realises that the scale of the invasion is beyond his comprehension. Meanwhile, Amy and Frederick are at the Admiralty, looking for George and trying to help organise a response. As the invasion continues, Frederick decides they should go to his summer house in Shoeburyness, and there they head to the beach to try to catch a boat away from Britain. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Sheffield United v Manchester United. Mark Chapman presents action from today's only Premier League fixture, held at Bramall Lane. The Blades have made an impressive start to their first top-flight campaign since the 2006/07 season, and will have been hoping to take the scalp of Ole Gunnar Solskjaer's men, who have struggled on their travels. With analysis by Darren Fletcher and Tim Cahill(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Liverpool at Meadow Park and Bristol City v Manchester City at Stoke Gifford Stadium(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: This time, Nick Knowles and the team transforms a home in Bromsgrove. Karis, a 28-year-old woman who has cerebral palsy, currently lives with her mother, Karen, but now her own health is deteriorating and it is making caring for Karis almost impossible. The family have decided that Karis should move in with her older sister Jo, but her entire home is inaccessible for Karis, so the team decides to build a large two-storey extension to make the house more suitable. Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass return to tackle more potential miscarriages of justice as they re-examine historic murder cases on behalf of the family of those convicted. In the first edition they investigate their oldest case yet, the murder of a canal boat passenger 180 years ago in which boatmen were convicted and publicly hanged(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts follow the development progress of a tenement flat in Glasgow, a two-bedroom terrace in Birmingham and a house in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Counterfeit contact lenses that are not sterile and risk wearers eyesight and the fake website racket that conned innocent Brits out of tens of millions of pounds(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: A driver's life hangs in the balance following a serious accident in one of the worst snowstorms in decades, while a passenger panics after getting stuck on an icy embankment(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall give advice to two teams scouring Ardingly in West Sussex for items to sell at auction, where one of Willy Wonka's golden tickets goes under the hammer(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear wriggles out of a tight corner, Daniel struggles to decide on a new car and Gareth's good mood is punctured. A police officer learns to look beyond her first impressions(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: DC Miranda Blake is in Palma de Mallorca to escort criminal turned supergrass Niall Taylor back to the UK, but things don't go according to plan. She ends up being paired with wisecracking German detective Max Winter by local police chief Ines Villegas, who authorises the pair to look into Taylor's gang.Crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a woman who wants to move to rural Devon on a £450,000 budget, and also heads to the seaside town of Dawlish to join in with the carnival(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team take on a mid-century coffee table, a wind-up panda that holds special family memories and an unusual kitchen utensil in the form of a coconut scraper(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Alliance Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews the leaders of the main parties in the run-up to the General Election(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A hungover Linda backs into another car while taking Ollie to school and drives away, convinced no one has seen. Louise is convinced Lisa is unwell after seeing her medication - but when Keanu tells Sharon, she is left feeling guilty. Ian holds an event to plug his Walford councillor campaign, only for his reputation to be compromised by an unwelcome visitor, and Ben is in high spirits when he manages to help Sonia(n)

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: The final part of Barra Best's journey starts at the point where the Lower Bann begins at the northern side of Lough Neagh. He receives a rowing lesson from Coleraine's Olympic medal-winning brothers Peter and Richard Chambers, goes in search of the elusive otter, and reflects on the beauty, scale and surprise of Northern Ireland's longest river as his trip ends(n)

programme.title: On the Frontline
programme.desc: During the Troubles nowhere was immune from the violence and fear inflicted on Northern Irish society, not even the schools. Through powerful and revealing interviews with teachers and former pupils, this documentary tells the story of how schools dealt with the traumatic experience of the era(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on how China runs its re-education camps, with more than a million people having been locked up in one of the biggest mass detentions in modern history. The investigation uncovers the reality of surveillance and abuse inside hundreds of new detention centres(n)

programme.title: Meat: A Threat to Our Planet?
programme.desc: Animal biologist Liz Bonnin investigates the environmental impact of animals being raised to supply the world's increasing demand for meat, and looks at efforts reduce the effects. She begins her journey in Texas, on a vast intensive farm with 50,000 cows, which emit huge amounts of methane, an incredibly potent greenhouse gas that is contributing to climate change. At a university in California, she hears of the efforts of scientists to better understand a cow's digestion so that they can reduce methane emissions(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by McFly's Dougie Poynter and chefs Brian Turner and Nisha Katona. Dr Hazel Wallace will be making perfect packed lunches and Oz Clarke continues his tour of some of Britain's best pubs(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Chef Gino D'Acampo tours his homeland by train, taking in some of some of the most scenic train journeys in Europe. He begins in the French Alpine town of Breil-sur-Roya, so he can travel into Italy in style. Once in his own country, he meets a doctor who does his rounds on horseback, and visits hazelnut groves where he prepares a chocolate cheesecake. In Turin, he fulfils a lifelong dream of racing on top of the world-famous Lingotto rooftop track and makes his final dish - fettuccine pasta with mascarpone and pork mince(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha joins forces with chef Zoe Adjonyoh to create a family feast from Africa. The menu comprises red bean stew for breakfast, Ghanaian spiced chicken kebabs for lunch, Zoe's peanut butter stew for dinner and Nadia's puff puff doughnuts for dessert(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.00 Betfair Steeple Chase, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.40 from Haydock Park. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.20 at Ascot. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Oli Bell, Luke Harvey, Alice Plunkett and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Westlife's Nicky Byrne, Olympian Amy Williams, Natalie Cassidy (EastEnders' Sonia Jackson) and Dave Myers of The Hairy Bikers pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents the show in which a group of men enters the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Today's contestants are a carnival dancer, a magician, a city worker and a sports therapist(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the star-studded version of the singing contest, in which the line-up of famous faces leave their comfort zones and take to the stage to perform - and while some have proved to be naturals, others have struggled somewhat. Simon Cowell mentors the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger is in charge of the Overs, and Louis Walsh handles the Unders, and as usual the judges are each allowed to send one act through to next week by pushing the golden buzzer, leaving the rest at the mercy of the voting public(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the celebrity survival challenge reality show. As the second week in the jungle begins, the campmates are already starting to feel tired and hungry - but they must still face the dreaded Bushtucker Trial(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio by comedian, author and Britain's Got Talent judge David Walliams and Edward Norton, who has recently directed his second movie, crime drama Motherless Brooklyn. Also dropping in are the stars of the new Charlie's Angels movie, Kristen Stewart, Naomi Scott and Ella Balinska, and Robbie Williams, who will be performing live(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill presents more mishaps caught on camera. A camel smashes a car window, a runaway speedboat causes a traffic accident and a drummer is crushed by a crucifix in church(n)

programme.title: Bang on the Money
programme.desc: Holiday camp entertainers The Hi-De-His from Somerset take on The Wolf Pack from Wolverhampton, battling it out to hit a specific target number 'bang on' in a series of physical and mental challenges. At the end of the contest, hosts Rickie Haywood-Williams and Melvin Odoom offer the most successful group a chance to win up to £40,000(n)

programme.title: Dare Master

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A fun run gives Mr Bean the chance to take revenge on the man who stole his parking space - but he has to hire a taxi to do it. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A hungry stork moves in with Bean and starts eating him out of house and home. His solution is to return it to the wild - but first he has to teach it to fly(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
